text: The surgical footage shows the dissection of the stomach, esophagus, and hernia sac from surrounding chest and abdominal soft tissue structures. The surgeon carefully identifies the vagal nerves to avoid injury during the mobilization of the hernia sac and esophagus. The esophagus is mobilized to ensure at least three centimeters of the distal esophagus lies within the abdomen, achieved through high mediastinal dissection.

The surgeon closes the enlarged diaphragmatic opening, or hiatus, with sutures to achieve a tension-free repair. A mesh is placed over the incision to reinforce the closure and minimize the risk of hiatal hernia recurrence.
